nameMeanings.name: Calver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Calver in Derbyshire, named in Old English with calf ‘calf’ + ofer ‘slope, ridge’.
possibly from Middle English calver ‘flecky-fleshed (of salmon)’, perhaps a nickname for someone with white flecks in their hair or mottled skin.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: Alice Calver was born about 1500, in England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Sir John Robert Calver. She married Sir Richard De Green in 1510, in England.
